methodology_sections: 2.3 Data Flow Tracing - Input flow, external data, asset movement, permission escalation
3.2 Business Logic Analysis - Economic incentives, edge cases, assumption violations, race conditions

path: Calls.execute receives _payload + _signature
BaseAuth.signatureValidation dispatches to BaseSig.recover or static sig check
BaseSig.recover parses signatureFlag, reads checkpoint/threshold
BaseSig.recoverBranch recursively parses merkle tree nodes
ecrecover/ERC1271/Sapient calls recover signer addresses
Weights accumulated, merkle root computed
imageHash computed from root + threshold + checkpoint + checkpointer
imageHash validated against stored or CREATE2-derived hash
transformations: ERC-2098 compact signature decomposed to r,s,v
ecrecover maps (hash,v,r,s) to address
Leaf hash computed per signer type
Merkle root built by sequential hashing of leaves/nodes
imageHash = fkeccak256(fkeccak256(fkeccak256(root, threshold), checkpoint), checkpointer)
sinks: imageHash compared to storage or CREATE2 address
data_flows.trust_changes: Raw bytes transition from untrusted user input to validated auth state after imageHash match
exposure_risks: opHash exposed to ERC1271/Sapient external contracts during validation
manipulation_risks: ecrecover returns address(0) on invalid sig - weight still added to tree
Unchecked arithmetic in recoverBranch can overflow weight after FLAG_SUBDIGEST sets it to max
LibBytes reads out-of-bounds return 0 silently instead of reverting
noChainId flag is set by signature, allowing cross-chain replay if signer opts in

path: SessionManager.recoverSapientSignature receives payload and encodedSignature
SessionSig.recoverSignature parses session configuration
Attestation list parsed and identity-signature verified
Per-call signatures recovered via ecrecover
Explicit calls validated via _validateExplicitCall with permission matching
Implicit calls validated via _validateImplicitCall with attestation check
Usage limits accumulated and validated via _validateLimitUsageIncrement
imageHash returned for sapient verification
transformations: Session configuration decoded into merkle tree with permissions, blacklist, identity signer
Attestation packed format decoded to struct
hashCallWithReplayProtection creates per-call hash for session signer verification
Permission rules applied with parameter masking and comparison
sinks: imageHash returned to BaseSig for merkle leaf computation
Usage limits written to storage via incrementUsageLimit
data_flows.trust_changes: Session signer trust bounded by permissions; identity signer authenticates attestations; attestation validates implicit mode
validation_points: Identity signer must match recovered signer from attestation signature
Session signer recovered from per-call signature must be non-zero
acceptImplicitRequest return value matched against magic
exposure_risks: acceptImplicitRequest external call to target contract in implicit mode
ERC1271 external call for recovery signature validation
manipulation_risks: Attestation index out of bounds checked but list size is uint8 limiting to 255
Implicit calls depend on target contract returning correct magic value
Session permissions matched by linear scan - first match wins

data_flows.source: Recovery signer submits payload via queuePayload
path: queuePayload validates signer's signature against recovery payload hash
Payload hash stored in timestampForQueuedPayload mapping
After timelock, recoverSapientSignatureCompact checks queued timestamp
_recoverBranch walks recovery merkle tree to find matching leaf
Returns merkle root as imageHash if verified
transformations: Recovery uses separate EIP712 domain ('Sequence Wallet - Recovery Mode')
recoveryPayloadHash computed with Recovery's domain separator
Payload.hashFor computed with Payload's domain separator for queue storage key
data_flows.trust_changes: Recovery signer gains time-delayed execution authority
exposure_risks: ERC1271 external call for contract signers
manipulation_risks: No mechanism to cancel queued payloads once queued
queuedPayloadHashes array grows unboundedly per signer per wallet
requiredDeltaTime only 24 bits (max ~194 days)

path: Payload.fromPackedCalls decodes packed bytes to Decoded struct
Nonce consumed via _consumeNonce
Signature validated via signatureValidation
_execute iterates calls with error handling
LibOptim.call or LibOptim.delegatecall dispatches
transformations: Global flag byte decoded to space, nonce, call count parameters
Per-call flag byte decoded to target, value, data, gas limit, delegate, fallback, behavior
data_flows.trust_changes: Validated payload gains execution authority in wallet context
exposure_risks: Delegatecall to external contracts in wallet context
Return data from failed calls exposed in events
manipulation_risks: onlyFallback logic depends on immediately preceding call's success - error flag resets each iteration
gasLimit=0 uses gasleft() which can be manipulated by relayers

data_flows.source: Any external call to wallet with unrecognized function selector
path: Hooks.fallback reads hook for selector
If hook exists, delegatecall to hook implementation
Hook executes in wallet's storage context
transformations: msg.data forwarded as-is to hook via delegatecall
sinks: Hook contract executes with full wallet storage access
data_flows.trust_changes: Hook gains full storage write access to wallet
validation_points: Hook must be registered via addHook (onlySelf)
exposure_risks: Malicious hook can modify any storage slot including implementation, imageHash, nonce
manipulation_risks: Hook can overwrite implementation address to hijack wallet
Hook can modify image hash to change signer configuration
No validation that hook contract is safe before delegatecall

data_flows.source: WebAuthn authenticator data and client JSON
path: Passkeys.recoverSapientSignatureCompact receives digest and encoded signature
_decodeSignature parses flags and extracts auth data
WebAuthn.verify validates authenticatorData flags and client JSON
sha256 computed over authenticatorData || sha256(clientDataJSON)
P256.verifySignature checks secp256r1 signature
_rootForPasskey computes merkle root from public key and metadata
transformations: Challenge base64-encoded and matched in clientDataJSON
authenticatorData flags checked for User Present and optionally User Verified
Double SHA256 hash for message construction
P256 signature verification with malleability check
sinks: Returns merkle root hash for sapient leaf in BaseSig tree
data_flows.trust_changes: WebAuthn assertion validated against P256 public key
validation_points: P256 signature validity with s <= N/2
exposure_risks: P256 precompile availability depends on chain (RIP-7212)
manipulation_risks: challengeIndex and typeIndex are user-provided - verified via string matching
Origin not verified - relies on authenticator enforcement
rpIdHash not verified - relies on authenticator enforcement

data_flows.source: Session usage from explicit call validation
path: SessionManager validates calls and tracks usage per signer
PermissionValidator.validatePermission accumulates cumulative rule values
Usage limits collected in SessionUsageLimits array
_validateLimitUsageIncrement verifies first call matches expected increment
incrementUsageLimit stores new usage amounts
transformations: Cumulative rule values summed with storage baseline
Value tracking hashed with signer and VALUE_TRACKING_ADDRESS
Expected increment call data encoded and hash-compared
data_flows.trust_changes: Usage limits enforced at validation time, persisted via first call
validation_points: incrementUsageLimit only allows increasing usage (no decrement)
First call must be increment with BEHAVIOR_REVERT_ON_ERROR
Call data hash must match expected increment
manipulation_risks: incrementUsageLimit is externally callable - anyone can increase their own wallet's usage (DoS by external party not possible since msg.sender is wallet key)
If first call fails, entire transaction reverts, preventing limit bypass

core_rules: Wallet authentication requires signer weights meeting threshold against verified imageHash
Nonces are per-space and must be sequential within each space
Session signers have bounded permissions: explicit (per-target rules) or implicit (attestation-based)
Recovery requires time-delayed payload queuing with minimum timestamp constraints
Image hash updates trigger implementation upgrade from Stage1 to Stage2
Hooks extend wallet via delegatecall, requiring self-call to install/remove
ERC4337 operations validate via isValidSignature treating userOpHash as a digest
economic_incentives: Session signers are bounded by value limits and cumulative parameter rules
Recovery timelock creates delay between queue and execution, allowing intervention
Checkpoint ordering in chained signatures enforces configuration update ordering
Usage limits prevent session signers from exceeding allocated resources across transactions
governance_mechanisms: Wallet configuration (imageHash) updated via weighted threshold signatures
Chained signatures enable configuration migration with checkpoint ordering
Checkpointer middleware can provide external state snapshots for validation
addHook/removeHook gated by onlySelf requiring full wallet auth

edge_cases.scenario: FLAG_SUBDIGEST sets weight to type(uint256).max, then subsequent signers in same branch add weight in unchecked arithmetic
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Weight should remain at or above type(uint256).max to always pass threshold
edge_cases.actual_behavior: In unchecked block, weight overflows to a small value (e.g., type(uint256).max + 1 = 0), potentially falling below threshold
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: A valid subdigest match in a configuration with other signers in the same merkle branch could cause signature rejection (DoS). An attacker with knowledge of the config structure could craft signatures that trigger this overflow, preventing legitimate operations. Even having a subdigest in a sub-branch that gets weight-summed in a parent with other signers triggers this.

edge_cases.scenario: ecrecover returns address(0) for invalid ECDSA signatures in FLAG_SIGNATURE_HASH and FLAG_SIGNATURE_ETH_SIGN
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Invalid signatures should not contribute weight to the signature tree
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Weight is unconditionally added for address(0), and a leaf node for address(0) is included in the merkle root
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: No direct exploit since address(0) leaf would mismatch the configured imageHash. However, if a configuration accidentally includes address(0) as a signer, any invalid signature would satisfy that leaf.

edge_cases.actual_behavior: calldataload beyond bounds returns zero-padded data, functions return 0 or zero values silently
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Malformed signatures or payloads could cause silent misinterpretation of data. For example, a truncated signature could be parsed with zero-filled values, potentially matching unexpected configurations. The impact depends on what downstream checks catch zero values.

edge_cases.scenario: Blacklist sorted check uses strict less-than, allowing duplicate entries
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Blacklist should contain unique sorted addresses
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Duplicate consecutive addresses pass the sort check (< not <=), and address(0) is allowed as first entry
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Duplicates waste gas and configuration space but don't affect blacklist correctness for binary search lookups

edge_cases.scenario: Recovery payloads cannot be cancelled once queued
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Wallet owner should be able to cancel pending recovery payloads
edge_cases.actual_behavior: No cancellation mechanism exists; once queued, a payload remains permanently and becomes executable after timelock
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If a recovery signer's key is compromised and used to queue a malicious payload, the wallet owner cannot prevent execution after the timelock expires, only by updating configuration to invalidate the recovery setup

edge_cases.scenario: SessionManager initializes totalValueUsed from storage but sessionUsageLimits array is sized to payload.calls.length, not unique signers count
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Array should be sized for unique signers
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Array is over-allocated (one entry per call), leading to empty entries that must be filtered later
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: No direct exploitation, but the linear scan for matching signer in sessionUsageLimits has O(n*m) complexity where n=calls and m=entries, potentially causing gas issues for large payloads

edge_cases.scenario: ExplicitSessionManager allows first call to address(this) without permission validation
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Only incrementUsageLimit should be allowed without explicit permission
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Any function on SessionManager can be called at index 0 without permission, but _validateLimitUsageIncrement later validates the call data matches expected increment
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If there are no usage limits and the first call targets SessionManager, _validateLimitUsageIncrement catches it. If there are limits, the call data must match. Effectively secure but relies on post-validation.

edge_cases.scenario: Chained signature processes checkpointer only on the last sub-signature
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Checkpointer state should be consistent across all sub-signatures
edge_cases.actual_behavior: In recoverChained, checkpointer is passed only to the last sub-signature (when nrindex == _signature.length), intermediate signatures use address(0) as checkpointer
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: By design - only the most recent (lowest checkpoint) configuration uses the checkpointer

edge_cases.scenario: Guest contract fallback executes arbitrary calls without authentication
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Guest is a deliberate unauthenticated dispatcher
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Any caller can execute calls through Guest, including value transfers if Guest holds ETH
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If ETH is accidentally sent to a Guest contract, anyone can drain it. No auth, no nonce, no replay protection for Guest operations.

edge_cases.scenario: Static signature timestamp check uses <= instead of <
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Timestamp should be a clear expiry boundary
edge_cases.actual_behavior: timestamp <= block.timestamp means the signature expires AT the timestamp, not after it. A timestamp of 0 would be immediately expired.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Off-by-one: a static signature with timestamp == block.timestamp would be rejected. Users must set timestamp > block.timestamp, which is the natural behavior.

edge_cases.scenario: Payload.fromPackedCalls uses calldatasize-based reading without boundary validation in LibBytes
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Malformed packed payload should revert cleanly
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Out-of-bounds reads in LibBytes return 0, leading to potentially valid but unintended Decoded payloads
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: A truncated payload could be parsed as having zero addresses (address(0)) for call targets, zero values, empty data - potentially causing unexpected self-calls or calls to address(0)

edge_cases.scenario: SessionManager bans BEHAVIOR_ABORT_ON_ERROR but allows BEHAVIOR_IGNORE_ERROR for non-first calls
edge_cases.expected_behavior: All session calls should have predictable execution outcomes
edge_cases.actual_behavior: BEHAVIOR_IGNORE_ERROR calls can fail silently after usage limits are committed via the first incrementUsageLimit call
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: A session signer could intentionally create calls that fail with IGNORE_ERROR, consuming usage limits without actual execution. This wastes the signer's own limits.

assumption_violations: BaseSig assumes weight will not overflow in unchecked block - violated when FLAG_SUBDIGEST sets weight to max and other signers exist
LibBytes assumes callers will only read within valid bounds - violated by malformed inputs producing silent zero reads
Recovery assumes block.timestamp is always > 0 - generally true on mainnet but could fail on test chains
WebAuthn assumes authenticators properly enforce origin and rpIdHash - violated if authenticator is compromised or misconfigured
SessionManager assumes session signers won't collude to bypass individual permission boundaries - mitigated by per-signer permissions but cumulative limits are per-signer not per-session
Stage1Auth assumes FACTORY and INIT_CODE_HASH remain constant - violated if factory is upgraded or wallet creation code changes
Hooks assume delegatecall targets are trustworthy - violated if a malicious hook is installed
game_theory_risks: Recovery signers can queue payloads that the wallet owner cannot cancel, creating a race between recovery execution and config update
Session signers with BEHAVIOR_IGNORE_ERROR can cause calls to fail silently while consuming shared usage limits
A bundle relayer could manipulate gas availability to cause gas-limited calls to fail (if gasLimit is set)
Implicit session targets (ISignalsImplicitMode implementers) could return incorrect magic values to block or allow operations
integration_risks: ERC1271 external calls during signature validation could be reentrancy vectors or gas-consuming
ISapient/ISapientCompact external calls during signature validation expose payload information
ICheckpointer external calls could return stale or manipulated snapshots
ISignalsImplicitMode.acceptImplicitRequest could revert, fail, or return incorrect values
P256 precompile (RIP-7212) availability varies across chains; fallback Solidity verifier must be deployed
ERC4337 EntryPoint trust assumption - entrypoint address is immutable and must be correct
calculation_errors: BaseSig.recoverBranch: unchecked weight overflow when FLAG_SUBDIGEST/FLAG_SIGNATURE_ANY_ADDRESS_SUBDIGEST sets weight=type(uint256).max and subsequent items add weight

PermissionValidator: cumulative rule previousUsage==0 check conflates 'first use in payload with 0 amount' and 'not yet seen in payload' - both fall through to storage read, which is correct but fragile

SessionSig.recoverConfiguration: maxPermissionsSize estimation uses integer division which could undercount if encoded.length is not a multiple of MIN_ENCODED_PERMISSION_SIZE - but this results in over-allocation not under-allocation, so it's safe

token_semantics.notes: Native ETH used for call values and gas. Value limits tracked per session signer via VALUE_TRACKING_ADDRESS sentinel.
Sentinel address used as key for native value limit tracking in session permissions, not a real token

invariants.name: nonce_sequential
invariants.description: For each nonce space, nonce values must be consumed sequentially (n, n+1, n+2, ...)

invariants.name: imageHash_authenticity
invariants.description: Stage1: imageHash derived from CREATE2(factory, imageHash, initCodeHash) == address(this). Stage2: imageHash stored in IMAGE_HASH_KEY must match recovered imageHash from signature

invariants.name: signature_weight_threshold
invariants.description: Recovered signer weight must be >= signature threshold for valid authentication. VIOLATED by unchecked overflow when subdigest + other signers in same branch.

invariants.name: chained_checkpoint_decreasing
invariants.description: Chained signatures must have strictly decreasing checkpoint values, ensuring configuration history ordering

invariants.name: session_usage_monotonic
invariants.description: Session usage limits can only increase (incrementUsageLimit reverts on decrease), preventing replay of lower usage values

invariants.name: recovery_timelock
invariants.description: Recovery payloads can only execute after queuing timestamp + requiredDeltaTime has elapsed AND queuing timestamp >= minTimestamp

invariants.name: reentrancy_guard
invariants.description: execute() and executeUserOp() are protected by nonReentrant modifier preventing recursive entry

invariants.name: session_no_self_call
invariants.description: Session-authorized transactions cannot call the wallet itself (call.to != wallet) or use delegate calls, preventing privilege escalation
